narrative.headline: 2020 Hyundai Sonata: fuel and propulsion lead the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 163 complaints with NHTSA. The fuel/propulsion system is the most reported area at 27%, followed by the electrical system (20%) and the engine (17%). No deaths are reported. Most of the few crashes, the single fire, and the two injuries cluster in the fuel/propulsion and engine areas, so those are worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.sample: AS THE VEHICLE WAS AT A COMPLETE STOP AND WAITING FOR A CHANCE TO GET IN TRAFFIC, THE CAR HESTIATED AND WOULD NOT RESPOND TO THE DEPRESS OF THE GAS PEDAL, FOR A SECOND OR TWO... VERY STRANGE INDEED!....IS IT A TRANSMISSION ISSUE OR SOMETHING ELSE....THE TRANSMISSION SHIFTS STRANG
issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 44 reports (27%), tied to 2 crashes, 1 fire, and 1 injury. Watch for hesitation or no response from the gas pedal.

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2020 HYUNDAI SONATA. THE CONTACT STATED THAT WHILE DRIVING AT APPROXIMATELY 55 MPH, THE VEHICLE SHIFTED INTO SAFE MODE AND LOST MOTIVE POWER. THE VEHICLE WAS TAKEN TO ST. CHARLES AUTOMOTIVE LOCATED AT 5601 VETERANS MEMORIAL PKWY, ST PETERS, MO 63376, (636)4
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 32 reports (20%), with 1 crash. Owners describe the car dropping into safe mode and losing power while driving.

issues.sample: VEHICLE ROUGH IDLED & EVENTUALLY SHUDDERED TO A STOP. THIS HAPPENED FOUR TIMES WHILE IN GEAR AND IDLING AT APPX 1000-1500 MILES. BETWEEN 1500-2000 MILES, VEHICLE SHUT DOWN WHILE DRIVING APPX 60MPH ON A COUNTY ROAD. AT APPX 2700 MILES, MPGS DRASTICALLY IMPACTED WHEN VEHICLE SUDDEN
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 28 reports (17%), with 1 fire, 1 crash, and 1 injury. Watch for rough idling or the engine shutting down while driving.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 22 reports (13%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries on record. A recurring complaint area without reported safety incidents.

issues.title: Seats
issues.blurb: The fewest of the top five: 14 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries on record. Owners describe a driver's seat weld failing.
